mnemonics: D.R.E.A.M.S. (Datasets, Results, Experiments, Architecture, Methods, Significance) - Use this acronym as a checklist for the key sections to evaluate in any DL review paper.
keyPoints: Focus on the problem the application solves and why DL is suited for it.
Critically analyze the chosen model architecture and its justification.
Scrutinize the experimental setup, metrics, and comparison to baselines.
commonMistakes: Confusing correlation with causation in the results.
Accepting the author's claims without checking the experimental evidence.
Overlooking the limitations and societal impact (e.g., bias in the dataset) discussed in the paper.
